# @andes/image

<br />

A component for rendering images with support for high resolution displays using srcSet.

<br />

## How to use

<br />

### Installation

> Follow the [general installation guide](../../docs/installation.md).

```bash

npm install --save @andes/image

```

### Scripts

> Follow the [general scripts usage guide](../../docs/scripts-usage.md) for configuration and how-tos.

Import the component in your page:

```jsx

import { Image } from '@andes/image';

```

## Public API

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `className` | Custom class. | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `height` | Custom height. | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `src` | Location image. | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `src2x` | Allows you to specify different versions of an image. | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `title` | <sup>1</sup>Label for screen readers. | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `width` | Custom width. | `string` | `undefined` |  |

> 1 - title should be provided for accessibility reasons.

## Examples

For full demo and configuration options, check out the [story for Image](https://andes.adminml.com/web/?selectedKind=Image).

### Basic Image

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { Image } from '@andes/image';

const MyComponent = () => (
  <div>
    <Image
      className="custom-class"
      src="https://http2.mlstatic.com/path/to/some/image.webp"
      title="alt by a11y"
    />
  </div>
);

export default MyComponent
```
